rung: R3 — truth-crop provider over the [D-49] split at n=192 (A3)
api: TruthCropProvider(cube|path, region, crop_size=64, scheme=DEFAULT_SCHEME, seed) -> .sample(n) / .crop_at(corner); module-level x_transform() replicating scripts/d75_corrected_metric_rescore.py:196-201 (X_FLOOR=1e-3, float64, floor-before-log)

d49_reuse_not_fork.refactor: extracted the fraction->voxel interval logic from SherwoodLoader.extract_rho_crops_split into module-level src.data.loader.region_voxel_interval; extract_rho_crops_split now delegates to it (behavior-identical; regression-gated)
d49_reuse_not_fork.sampling: rejection sampling mirrors extract_rho_crops_split: one RNG advanced through accepts AND rejects; split axis non-wrapping by acceptance; transverse axes periodic

n192_vs_768_documentation.scheme_is_fraction_based: HeldoutSplitScheme stores normalized fractions (0.7/0.85, axis 0) — lattice-independent; region_voxel_interval applies int() truncation per lattice
n192_vs_768_documentation.crop64_consequences_at_192: train corners on axis 0 in [0, 70] -> 71 distinct offsets (spec's ~70); val/test/heldout widths 29/29/58 < 64 -> constructor refuses 64-crops there by design (test-region eval is sliding-window full-box masked to test voxels per spec §2(a))
n192_vs_768_documentation.truncation_note: 134/192 = 0.6979 vs 537/768 = 0.6992 — boundaries at different lattices agree to within one voxel of the exact fraction; both are floor(0.7*n)

coverage: x-transform <=1e-12 (bit-exact) vs d75 expression + clamped fraction
determinism under seed / stream advance / seed divergence

regression_gate.tests/test_rho_crop_extraction.py + test: 10 passed, 1 failed in first post-refactor run; the single failure (test_default_cache_dir_resolves_to_d_drive) VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING via git-stash rerun on the pre-refactor tree (asserts the retired Windows D:-drive cache default, fixed in loader 2026-06-25; platform-impossible on macOS). Test updated to assert the documented repo-relative default; suites re-run green (see final status below).
